name: Halvah Shortbread
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Toasted pecans or walnuts (chopped or ground)
A few whole pecan or walnut halves
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Using a food processor or by hand, cream together the butter and tahini. Add the salt and brown sugar, blending until smooth. Gradually incorporate the flour, stirring until well combined. Mix in the chopped nuts. The dough will be stiff.
Lightly butter two 7-inch pie plates or shallow baking pans. Press the dough evenly over the bottom and sides of the pans to a thickness of no more than 1/4 inch. Decorate with whole nuts on top.
Bake the shortbread for 15 minutes, then check it frequently every couple of minutes. Remove as soon as the edges are golden brown, being careful not to overbake.
While still warm, cut each shortbread into 8 or 10 wedges in the pan; do not wait until it is cool, as it will crumble. This shortbread pairs well with spearmint or spiced tea, espresso, or Turkish coffee.
